meta.method_note: Simple pooling heuristic. Not formal hierarchical Bayesian shrinkage — no informative prior or posterior uncertainty is computed. Per-vehicle λ_v loaded from cell_to_field_temporal_report.json (train-window OLS, no new fit). No flooring of negatives: flooring would bias the mean upward and be inconsistent with how the fleet-median baseline (0.018476) was computed (raw np.median, no floor).
meta.source_lambda_v: cell_to_field_temporal_report.json — train-window OLS fits (50% of each vehicle timeline)

pre_registration.qualitative_expectation: λ_pool (mean=0.024895) is slightly lower than frozen scalar 0.02639332 (Δλ≈0.0015) and substantially higher than all-20 fleet median 0.018476 because Tier 2 over-samples high-fade vehicles (V08=0.084, V09=0.063). Expected Tier 3 RMSE outcome: null result (Δλ≈0.0015 → ΔSOH≈0.001-0.002, within fleet noise floor of 0.02-0.04).

tier1_2_fit.lambda_used_note: arithmetic mean of raw V01-V09 λ_v (no floor)

B_fleet.leakage_note: LEAKAGE-CONTAMINATED — NOT a valid held-out baseline. lambda_global=0.018476 is the median of per-vehicle lambda_v across ALL 20 vehicles including V10-V20 (Tier 3). Evaluating this lambda on the same Tier 3 vehicles it was computed from is partly tautological. Its lower RMSE (0.03465) is not interpretable as model quality.

B_pool.source: V01-V09 pooled mean, no floor (this module)

tier3_evaluation.split: test window: t > 0.50 * t_max per vehicle (same split as cell_to_field_temporal.py)
tier3_evaluation.metric: trajectory RMSE on test-window ΔSOH timeseries

B_fleet.leakage_flag: LEAKAGE-CONTAMINATED — NOT a valid held-out baseline. See baselines.B_fleet.leakage_note.

representativeness_note: Tier 2 (V05-V09) is not representative of the full fleet. V08 (lambda=0.084) and V09 (lambda=0.063) are extreme high-fade outliers; V05 and V07 are negative-lambda. The V01-V09 pool mean (0.025) sits well above the all-20 fleet median (0.018) as a result.
future_work_note: A genuinely valid fleet-wide prior would require either (a) leave-one-vehicle-out cross-validation across all 20 vehicles, or (b) an external/held-out fleet. Neither is computed here.
verdict: NULL_RESULT (valid comparison only: B_pool vs B_sei). B_fleet's RMSE advantage (0.03465 vs 0.03623-0.03682) is not interpretable as model quality — its lambda was computed partly from the same Tier 3 vehicles it is evaluated on (data leakage). The only valid comparison in this experiment is B_pool vs B_sei, both computed entirely from Tier 1+2 (V01-V09) with zero Tier 3 contamination. That comparison is the null result: delta_lambda=0.0015 gives delta_RMSE=0.00059, within the fleet noise floor of 0.02-0.04 SOH units. Pre-registered expectation confirmed.
